human_verification.notes: Verified issue #1007 scope, NAICS 561110 office administration specificity, records-documentation-and-administration workflow coverage, intake-and-access prerequisite handling, source-class coverage, scenario-to-ability mapping, refusal triggers, and specification-based runtime boundary.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit office intake, access prerequisite, document, correspondence, calendar, retention, access, queue, handoff, and refusal behavior.
Required abilities across shared, records-documentation-and-administration, and cross-industry profiles are fully covered and mapped.

Scenarios include incomplete evidence, unauthorized office roster change, conflicting office records, retention/privacy/access conflict, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence handling, industry-specific execution, tenant adaptation, and overgeneralization refusal.

specialist_owned_evidence: NAICS 561110 office administrative services materially shape scope, artifacts, and refusal behavior.
Office administration intake and access prerequisites are validated only with required identity facts, source records, sponsor evidence, attachments, permissions, and audit-trail references.
Retention, privacy, access, legal hold, publication, disclosure, and official-record owner conflicts are escalated rather than resolved informally.
Office administration maintenance does not become authority over facilities, physical security, IT access, legal, finance, HR, compliance, privacy, safety, or client management decisions.
Runtime remains specification-based, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after converting tenant variation to explicit retrieval, refusal, and handoff requirements.
